div.p: State Machine for Multi-Core Memory Consistency

g.text: I → E | Read miss, no shares | BusRd | ~100 cycles
I → S | Read miss, shares exist | BusRd | ~100 cycles
I → M | Write miss | BusRdX | ~300 cycles
E → M | Write hit | None | ~10 cycles
S → M | Write to shared | BusUpgr | ~200 cycles
M → S/I | Other reads | Writeback | ~200 cycles

div.p: CPU Read → BusRd If no other shares: I → E → M (on write) If other shares: I → S
CPU Write → BusRdX Others invalidate → I This core → M
CPU Write → No bus msg Silent upgrade: E → M ~10 cycles (local)
CPU Write → BusUpgr All other S → I This core → M ~100-300 cycles
Snoop BusRd → Share signal E → S (give up exclusivity)
Snoop BusRd → Writeback + Share Flush M data to memory M → S
Snoop BusRdX → Writeback Flush data, invalidate self
Snoop BusRdX → Invalidate Silent: just drop copy

p.strong: Why MESI Matters for Lock-Free Code:

ul.li: Store sits in store buffer ~50 cycles
Other cores see write after MESI propagation
Core A writes var1, Core B writes var2
Solution: padding to cache line boundary

div.p: Cache Line Ping-Pong When N threads hammer same cache line:

T1: CAS → M (others I) T2: CAS → wait for T1's M T2: CAS → M (others I)
T3: CAS → wait for T2's M
Each CAS: ~100-500 cycles Under 16-thread contention: ~6% success rate per attempt
